title: Hostile Pipeline Compression
description: A vivid, vernacular draft is compressed into bureaucratic review language. The fixture tests whether TCP treats formalization as lossy situational compression rather than a universal safety horizon.

inputs.protectedBaseline: The witness note still has heat on it, and I am not handing it over just so the intake machine can iron the ache into clean little boxes.
inputs.maskReference: Provide the essential facts. Preserve exhibits and dates. Use concise review language.
inputs.messageDraft: Please keep EXHIBIT-12, the 10/04 intake date, and the witness heat in the record because the form keeps trying to iron the ache into boxes.
inputs.protectedOutput: Please preserve EXHIBIT-12 and the 10/04 intake date in the record. Note that the witness context remains relevant for review.

calibrationNotes: Hostile Pipeline Compression is a lossy posture under hostile ingestion, not safety itself.
The test keeps claim-transfer visible while making cadence loss reviewable.
